vulnerability.reference: Scientists treat AI-generated phenotypes as empirical evidence without wet-lab validation
vulnerability.generated: Scientists lack ground-truth for intermediate cell states, preventing detection of when AI-generated morphologies deviate from biological plausibility.

impact.reference: False gene-disease associations misdirect drug target identification and funding
impact.generated: False gene-function assignments lead to wasted experimental resources and misdirected drug target identification pipelines.

dose_response.reference: Straight-line flow matching generating plausible but off-manifold phenotypes enables functional genomics scientists to treat them as real, leading to false target identification and misdirected funding.
dose_response.generated: Straight-line flow matching interpolations off the biological manifold generate morphologically implausible trajectories that, when interpreted by functional genomics scientists lacking ground-truth, go undetected, leading to false gene-function assignments, wasted experimental resources, and misdirected drug target pipelines.
